stimulus: Roughly forty days each spring, when nutrient-rich currents from the Humboldt upwelling reach the shallow waters off Peru’s Paracas Peninsula, the result is a vast plankton bloom, its glow at night so wide, so steady, and so ______blank that fishermen have nicknamed the stretch “the Quiet Lantern.”
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the punctuation of an integrated relative clause. No punctuation is needed before the "that" clause because its content ("that fishermen…'the Quiet Lantern'") is integral to the meaning of the coordinated adjectival phrase ("so wide, so steady, and so even") it modifies.

Choices A, B, and C are incorrect because no comma, colon, or semicolon should separate a coordinated adjectival phrase from a restrictive relative clause that modifies it.
